implementation_tasks.details: Create a runtime plugin catalog and per-package registries so Nelson packages can be discovered and identified alongside core, overlay, and external skill sources. This is the first commit-ready slice of the Nelson dynamic integration plan.

implementation_tasks.details: Teach skill selection to consult Nelson package registries after core skills and before fallback. Selected skills should carry source labels so logs explain why a Nelson or core skill was chosen.

implementation_tasks.title: Make Nelson skill selection source-aware

implementation_tasks.details: Add Nelson relevance checks and package activation logic so package-local hooks, rules, and workflows load when Nelson is relevant or explicitly requested, while core runtime order remains authoritative.

implementation_tasks.details: Extend runtime self-tests to cover Nelson relevance, package-local loading, and non-Nelson fallback behavior, then update the Nelson integration docs and Superpowers Codex docs to explain the shared overlay model.

implementation_tasks.title: Add Nelson end-to-end tests and docs

closeout_criteria: All six linked Nelson parity-gap tasks are completed and verified.
The Codex runtime can select, load, and preserve Nelson plugin state end to end.
The recommendation is only closed after review evidence and task completion evidence are present.
recommendations.details:
Gap matrix:
- Commands: missing. The public Claude Code Nelson plugin exposes user-facing command surfaces; the Codex Nelson package currently relies on runtime selection and package-local loading only.
- Agent/subagent orchestration: missing. The public plugin behavior implies coordinated agent workflows; the Codex Nelson package does not yet define Nelson-specific agents or managed background jobs.
- MCP/tool integrations: missing. The public plugin model supports MCP servers and app integrations; the Codex Nelson package currently has no Nelson-specific MCP or app wiring.
- Coordination log/state: partial. Codex records plugin_id, plugin_source, plugin_context, and plugin_package, but it does not yet provide a Nelson-native captain's log / action-state ledger.
- Workflow breadth: partial. Codex has one conversion workflow and package-local registries; the public Nelson plugin appears to be a general coordination framework for recurring task orchestration.

Remediation plan:
1. Define Nelson command surfaces for the most common coordination operations.
2. Add Nelson-specific agents or subagent entrypoints for orchestration and background tasks.
3. Introduce MCP/app integrations where needed to support external coordination steps.
4. Add a Nelson coordination log and action-state ledger separate from the generic runtime state snapshot.
5. Expand mission coverage beyond the conversion workflow into reusable coordination flows.
6. Add tests that prove Nelson can be selected, load its package context, execute its commands, and preserve coordination state end to end.

Priority rationale: high. This is a functional parity gap versus the public plugin behavior, not a minor enhancement.
recommendations.summary: Add the missing plugin-class capabilities needed for Nelson to behave like the public Claude Code Nelson package.
recommendations.title: Close Nelson Claude Code parity gaps
